generatedSentence: In the sprawling urban dystopia, Elara, a prodigy who had majored in linguistic data streams, operated as a phantom operator. Her unique handwriting and prankish spirit were an oddity in a world where identical haircuts and mundane pursuits were the norm. She was still a virgin to direct confrontation, but her radar detected a pervasive malaise. She knew exactly the system’s flaw, a constant cliffhanger threatening to unjoint society. Many dismissed her as a killjoy or one of the city's many nutjobs, but her persistence was adamant. She saw the measurement of its decay in old films, understanding the true karma of this crumbling world. She rejoins her wingman, a former jury member, after a visit to the underground clubs where he was known to be cranky. Their journeys led them to uncover a moneymaking scheme, a mini-empire built on fraudulent energy credits. This intricate web, spanning a mere inch of digital space, was designed to outplay legitimate businesses. Elara, a brilliant keyboardist in her downtime, felt a strange epiphany while examining the code, revealing the true validity of their suspicions. She realized the system's negation of ethical conduct was a programmed feature. They found a furry koala plush toy, a strange oddity left behind by an intruder in the network's storage facility. The elderly chief programmer, now a broken man, offered a chilling theoretical explanation of how the corrupt few govern the masses. He spoke of the looming mirage of hope, a cruel trick to keep the voters complacent. It was a funny, yet terrifying, revelation about the depths of their depravity. The takeoff of their plan involved infiltrating a high-security naval facility, hidden nowhere and everywhere within the city’s dense fabric. They had to detonate a data bomb, a digital lion in the machine, to expose the corruption. Their path was complicated by a group of aggressive mutants, henchmen of the regime, who tried to outplay them at every turn. Elara’s ancestor had warned of such dangers in ancient texts. She carried a small vial of vinegar, a symbolic deterrent, as they navigated the facility’s upholstery-lined corridors. A quick-thinking maneuver by her wingman had an enemy ejected from the data hub. Elara, focused and adamant, recorded the data, preparing for the grand reveal at the city's banquet. The thought of the cheaters being exposed fueled her; this was their epic moment, a cliffhanger for the entire city. The banquet hall, filled with the city's elite, was the stage for their final act. The projector, powered by coconut oil, flickered to life, showing the recorded evidence. There was no longer anywhere to hide for the corrupt officials. One of the nutjobs from the council, a notorious pickpocketing enthusiast, tried to disrupt the presentation, but his efforts were in vain. The truth, strong as pure juice, spread through the room. The elderly citizens, long oppressed, watched with dawning hope. Elara felt a sense of victory, not just for herself, but for her family. The city’s urns of injustice were finally breaking, showering the powerful with their own powder. The turtles in the grand fountain of the veranda seemed to swim with renewed vigor. The vulgarity of the corrupt regime was laid bare, ensuring their journey to jail. The city found shelter in the truth, beginning a new time under the clear, heather-colored skies. The truth, once a mirage, now solidified into sands of justice.
panelSentences: In the sprawling urban dystopia, Elara, a prodigy who had majored in linguistic data streams, operated as a phantom operator. Her unique handwriting and prankish spirit were an oddity in a world where identical haircuts and mundane pursuits were the norm. She was still a virgin to direct confrontation, but her radar detected a pervasive malaise. She knew exactly the system’s flaw, a constant cliffhanger threatening to unjoint society. Many dismissed her as a killjoy or one of the city's many nutjobs, but her persistence was adamant. She saw the measurement of its decay in old films, understanding the true karma of this crumbling world. She rejoins her wingman, a former jury member, after a visit to the underground clubs where he was known to be cranky.

Their journeys led them to uncover a moneymaking scheme, a mini-empire built on fraudulent energy credits. This intricate web, spanning a mere inch of digital space, was designed to outplay legitimate businesses. Elara, a brilliant keyboardist in her downtime, felt a strange epiphany while examining the code, revealing the true validity of their suspicions. She realized the system's negation of ethical conduct was a programmed feature. They found a furry koala plush toy, a strange oddity left behind by an intruder in the network's storage facility. The elderly chief programmer, now a broken man, offered a chilling theoretical explanation of how the corrupt few govern the masses. He spoke of the looming mirage of hope, a cruel trick to keep the voters complacent. It was a funny, yet terrifying, revelation about the depths of their depravity.

The takeoff of their plan involved infiltrating a high-security naval facility, hidden nowhere and everywhere within the city’s dense fabric. They had to detonate a data bomb, a digital lion in the machine, to expose the corruption. Their path was complicated by a group of aggressive mutants, henchmen of the regime, who tried to outplay them at every turn. Elara’s ancestor had warned of such dangers in ancient texts. She carried a small vial of vinegar, a symbolic deterrent, as they navigated the facility’s upholstery-lined corridors. A quick-thinking maneuver by her wingman had an enemy ejected from the data hub. Elara, focused and adamant, recorded the data, preparing for the grand reveal at the city's banquet. The thought of the cheaters being exposed fueled her; this was their epic moment, a cliffhanger for the entire city.

The banquet hall, filled with the city's elite, was the stage for their final act. The projector, powered by coconut oil, flickered to life, showing the recorded evidence. There was no longer anywhere to hide for the corrupt officials. One of the nutjobs from the council, a notorious pickpocketing enthusiast, tried to disrupt the presentation, but his efforts were in vain. The truth, strong as pure juice, spread through the room. The elderly citizens, long oppressed, watched with dawning hope. Elara felt a sense of victory, not just for herself, but for her family. The city’s urns of injustice were finally breaking, showering the powerful with their own powder. The turtles in the grand fountain of the veranda seemed to swim with renewed vigor. The vulgarity of the corrupt regime was laid bare, ensuring their journey to jail. The city found shelter in the truth, beginning a new time under the clear, heather-colored skies. The truth, once a mirage, now solidified into sands of justice.
rawPanelSentences: In the sprawling urban **dystopia**, Elara, a **prodigy** who had **majored** in **linguistic** data streams, operated as a phantom **operator**. Her unique **handwriting** and **prankish** spirit were an **oddity** in a world where identical **haircuts** and mundane pursuits were the norm. She was still a **virgin** to direct confrontation, but her **radar** detected a pervasive malaise. She knew **exactly** the system’s flaw, a constant **cliffhanger** threatening to unjoint society. Many dismissed her as a **killjoy** or one of the city's many **nutjobs**, but her **persistence** was **adamant**. She saw the **measurement** of its decay in old **films**, understanding the true **karma** of this crumbling world. She **rejoins** her **wingman**, a former **jury** member, after a visit to the underground **clubs** where he was known to be **cranky**.

Their **journeys** led them to uncover a **moneymaking** scheme, a **mini**-empire built on **fraudulent** energy credits. This intricate web, spanning a mere **inch** of digital space, was designed to **outplay** legitimate businesses. Elara, a brilliant **keyboardist** in her downtime, felt a strange **epiphany** while examining the code, revealing the true **validity** of their suspicions. She realized the system's **negation** of ethical conduct was a programmed feature. They found a **furry** **koala** plush toy, a strange **oddity** left behind by an **intruder** in the network's **storage** facility. The **elderly** chief programmer, now a broken man, offered a chilling **theoretical** explanation of how the corrupt few **govern** the masses. He spoke of the looming **mirage** of hope, a cruel trick to keep the **voters** complacent. It was a **funny**, yet terrifying, revelation about the depths of their depravity.

The **takeoff** of their plan involved infiltrating a high-security **naval** facility, hidden **nowhere** and everywhere within the city’s dense fabric. They had to **detonate** a data bomb, a digital **lion** in the machine, to expose the corruption. Their path was complicated by a group of aggressive **mutants**, henchmen of the regime, who tried to **outplay** them at every turn. Elara’s **ancestor** had warned of such dangers in ancient texts. She carried a small vial of **vinegar**, a symbolic deterrent, as they navigated the facility’s **upholstery**-lined corridors. A quick-thinking maneuver by her **wingman** had an enemy **ejected** from the data hub. Elara, focused and **adamant**, recorded the data, preparing for the grand reveal at the city's **banquet**. The thought of the **cheaters** being exposed fueled her; this was their **epic** moment, a **cliffhanger** for the entire city.

The **banquet** hall, filled with the city's elite, was the stage for their final act. The projector, powered by **coconut** oil, flickered to life, showing the **recorded** evidence. There was no longer **anywhere** to hide for the corrupt officials. One of the **nutjobs** from the council, a notorious **pickpocketing** enthusiast, tried to disrupt the presentation, but his efforts were in vain. The truth, strong as pure **juice**, spread through the room. The **elderly** citizens, long oppressed, watched with dawning hope. Elara felt a sense of **victory**, not just for herself, but for her **family**. The city’s **urns** of injustice were finally breaking, showering the powerful with their own **powder**. The **turtles** in the grand fountain of the **veranda** seemed to swim with renewed vigor. The **vulgarity** of the corrupt regime was laid bare, ensuring their journey to **jail**. The city found **shelter** in the truth, beginning a new **time** under the clear, **heather**-colored skies. The truth, once a **mirage**, now solidified into **sands** of justice.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A female hacker with bright pink hair, wearing augmented reality goggles and a sleek, armored trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, seen from a high-angle, hunched over a glowing holographic keyboard in a dimly lit, high-tech hideout, surrounded by swirling data projections. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, close-up, expression of intense focus as she points a small, glowing device at a massive, intricate server rack, with digital locks breaking apart. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist and her wingman (a burly figure with a cybernetic arm), mid-action, fighting off a group of shadowy, cloaked figures in a corridor filled with neon-lit pipes, low-angle shot looking up at the action. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, full shot, standing triumphantly on a grand stage, bathed in spotlight, as a large screen behind her displays incriminating data, a crowd of silhouetted figures looking up in awe from a low-angle perspective.

definitions.definition: The sum total of a person's actions, which determine the person's next incarnation in samsara, the cycle of death and rebirth.
A force or law of nature which causes one to reap what one sows; destiny; fate.
A distinctive feeling, aura, or atmosphere.
(Reddit) A score assigned to a user of a discussion forum, indicating the popularity of their posts with other users.
The act of cutting of the hair, often done professionally by a barber, hair stylist, or beautician.
The style into which the hair is cut.
In a bankruptcy proceeding, the proportional reduction in the debt that will be paid to each creditor, based on an evaluation of the total debt owed and the total assets of the debtor.
The difference between the value of a loan and the value of its collateral.
Someone who is crazy (insane).
definitions.example: Some nut job from the government actually thought he could tax our moonshine!

definitions.definition: A silvery elemental metal with an atomic number of 28 and symbol Ni.
A coin worth 5 cents.
(by extension) Five dollars.
(by extension) Five hundred dollars.
(sometimes the nickel or the hot nickel) Interstate 5, a highway that runs along the west coast of the United States.
A playing card with the rank of five
A five-year prison sentence.
A defensive formation with five defensive backs, one of whom is a nickelback, instead of the more common four.
To plate with nickel.
A person who has never had sexual intercourse, or sometimes, one who has never engaged in any sexual activity at all.
(early Christian Church) a woman noted for religious piety who has never been married.
One who has never used or experienced a specified thing.
definitions.example: I've never eaten tofu before – you could say I'm a tofu virgin.

definitions.definition: Any of several species of gossamer-winged butterflies of the family Lycaenidae.
A female insect producing eggs from which young are hatched, though there has been no fecundation by a male; a parthenogenetic insect.
(usually not comparable) In a state of virginity; chaste, not having had sexual intercourse.
Of a physical object, untouched.
Not yet cultivated, explored, or exploited by humans or humans of certain civilizations.
definitions.example: The virgin lands of the Americas were awaiting the Europeans.

definitions.definition: Of olive oil, obtained by mechanical means, so that the oil is not altered.
(usually not comparable) Of mixed drinks, not containing alcohol.
definitions.example: a virgin daiquiri

definitions.definition: A pilot partner of another, a pilot who flies in the same wing or squadron.
(by extension) a friend who accompanies one to offer (or receive) support, especially in flirting with love interests
Of or relating to language.
Of or relating to linguistics.
Relating to a computer language.
To concentrate on a particular area of study as a student in a college or university
definitions.example: I have decided to major in mathematics.

definitions.definition: One who operates.
A telecommunications facilitator whose job is to establish temporary network connections.
A function or other mapping that carries variables defined on a domain into another variable or set of variables in a defined range.
The game of Chinese whispers.
A person who is adept at making deals or getting results, especially one who uses questionable methods.
A member of a military special operations unit.
The administrator of a channel or network on IRC.
A symbol that represents a construct in a programming language and differs from a normal function in its syntax.
A kind of expression that enters into an a-bar movement dependency and is said to bind a variable.
definitions.example: In the sentence "What did Bill say he wants to buy?", "what" is an operator, binding a phonetically empty variable.

definitions.definition: An odd or strange thing or opinion.
A strange person; an oddball.
Strangeness.
Trees in a forest regarded as a source of wood.
(outside North America) Wood that has been pre-cut and is ready for use in construction.
A heavy wooden beam, generally a whole log that has been squared off and used to provide heavy support for something such as a roof.
definitions.example: the timbers of a ship

definitions.definition: Material for any structure.
The wooden stock of a rifle or shotgun.
A certain quantity of fur skins (as of martens, ermines, sables, etc.) packed between boards; in some cases forty skins, in others one hundred and twenty. Also timmer, timbre.
To fit with timbers.
definitions.example: timbering a roof

definitions.definition: To construct, frame, build.
To light or land on a tree.
To make a nest.
To surmount as a timber does.
Used by loggers to warn others that a tree being felled is falling.
A group of individuals chosen from the general population to hear and decide a case in a court of law.
A group of judges in a competition.
The audience attending the first night of a performance, whose reaction may determine whether it succeeds or fails.
To judge by means of a jury.
A person who is anti-fun, or prevents others from having fun.
A vision of a future that is a corrupted (usually beyond recognition) utopian society.
A miserable, dysfunctional state or society that has a very poor standard of living.
Anatomical tissue that is not found in its usual place.
definitions.example: The patient suffers from adrenal dystopia.

definitions.definition: To write something manually, normally used to emphasise that it is not being typed.
The act or process of writing done with the hand, rather than typed or word-processed.
Text that was written by hand.
The characteristic writing of a particular person.
(by extension) A characteristic trait or hallmark by which somebody may be recognised.
An association of members joining together for some common purpose, especially sports or recreation.
A heavy stick intended for use as a weapon or plaything.
A joint charge of expense, or any person's share of it; a contribution to a common fund.
An establishment that provides staged entertainment, often with food and drink, such as a nightclub.
definitions.example: She was sitting in a jazz club, sipping wine and listening to a bass player's solo.

definitions.definition: A black clover shape (♣), one of the four symbols used to mark the suits of playing cards.
Any set of people with a shared characteristic.
definitions.example: Michael stood you up?  Welcome to the club.

definitions.definition: A club sandwich.
The slice of bread in the middle of a club sandwich.
To hit with a club.
definitions.example: He clubbed the poor dog.

definitions.definition: To join together to form a group.
To combine into a club-shaped mass.
definitions.example: a medical condition with clubbing of the fingers and toes

definitions.definition: To go to nightclubs.
definitions.example: We went clubbing in Ibiza.

definitions.definition: To pay an equal or proportionate share of a common charge or expense.
To raise, or defray, by a proportional assessment.
definitions.example: to club the expense

definitions.definition: To drift in a current with an anchor out.
To throw, or allow to fall, into confusion.
To unite, or contribute, for the accomplishment of a common end.
definitions.example: to club exertions

definitions.definition: To turn the breech of (a musket) uppermost, so as to use it as a club.
One of the four suits of playing cards, marked with the symbol ♣.
A game in which questions are asked for the purpose of enabling the questioners to discover a word or thing previously selected by two persons who answer the questions.
Weak, unwell.
(of a machine, etc.) Not in good working condition.
Grouchy, grumpy, irritable; easily upset.
definitions.example: He got home from a long day at work tired and cranky.

definitions.definition: Not in perfect mental working order; eccentric, peculiar.
Full of spirit; spirited.
Likely to be affected by or to experience something.
definitions.example: He's subject to sneezing fits.

definitions.definition: Conditional upon.
definitions.example: The local board sets local policy, subject to approval from the State Board.

definitions.definition: Placed or situated under; lying below, or in a lower situation.
Placed under the power of another; owing allegiance to a particular sovereign or state.
Given to or characterized by impishness or playfulness; mischievous.
An extraordinary thing seen as an omen; a portent.
An extraordinary occurrence or creature; an anomaly, especially a monster; a freak.
An amazing or marvellous thing; a wonder.
A wonderful example of something.
An extremely talented person, especially a child.
The act of measuring.
Magnitude (or extent or amount) determined by an act of measuring.
A thin layer of some substance; a pellicle; a membranous covering, causing opacity.
definitions.example: a clear plastic film for wrapping food

definitions.definition: A medium used to capture images in a camera.
A movie.
Cinema; movies as a group.
A slender thread, such as that of a cobweb.
To record (activity, or a motion picture) on photographic film.
definitions.example: A Hollywood studio was filming on location in NYC.

definitions.definition: To cover or become covered with a thin skin or pellicle.
(manner) without approximation; precisely.
definitions.example: He divided the coins exactly in half.

definitions.definition: (focus) Used to provide emphasis.
definitions.example: He did it that way exactly to prove the point.

definitions.definition: Signifies agreement or recognition.
definitions.example: So you're saying that we have only three days left? — Yes, exactly!

definitions.definition: A method of detecting distant objects and determining their position, velocity, or other characteristics by analysis of sent radio waves (usually microwaves) reflected from their surfaces
A type of system using such method, differentiated by platform, configuration, frequency, power, and other technical attributes.
An installation of such a system or of the transmitting and receiving apparatus.
A superior ability to detect something.
definitions.example: His sensitive radar for hidden alliances keeps him out of trouble.

definitions.definition: To scan with radar, or as if with radar.
To join again; to unite after separation.
To come, or go, again into the presence of; to join the company of again.
To state in reply; -- followed by an object clause.
: To answer to a reply.
To answer, as the defendant to the plaintiff's replication.

In US patent law To re-insert a patent claim, typically after allowance of a patent application, applied to patent claims that had been withdrawn from examination under a restriction requirement, based on rejoinder .
A set amount of travelling, seen as a single unit; a discrete trip, a voyage.
Any process or progression likened to a journey, especially one that involves difficulties or personal development.
definitions.example: my journey of dealing with grief

definitions.definition: A day.
A day's travelling; the distance travelled in a day.
A day's work.
The weight of finished coins delivered at one time to the Master of the Mint.
(collective) A group of giraffes.
To travel, to make a trip or voyage.
Someone who plays a keyboard.
An ending or stopping point calculated to leave a story unresolved, in order to create suspense.
(by extension) An outcome which is awaited with keen anticipation, especially one which is delayed for a period of time or which is not known until the last minute.
The craft or business of upholstering furniture.
The materials used in upholstering furniture.
To dislocate.
To disjoint.
A strange, odd, eccentric person.
An insane, possibly dangerous person.
An imaginary rock or mineral of impenetrable hardness; a name given to the diamond and other substances of extreme hardness.
An embodiment of impregnable hardness.
A lodestone.
(said of people and their conviction) Firm; unshakeable; unyielding; determined.
(of an object) Very difficult to break, pierce, or cut.
The property of being persistent.
definitions.example: You've got to admire his persistence. He's asked her out every day for a month even though she keeps turning him down.

definitions.definition: Of data, the property of continuing to exist after the termination of the program.
definitions.example: Once written to a disk file, the data has persistence: it will still be there tomorrow when we run the next program.

definitions.definition: Continuation of the previous day's weather (particularly temperature and precipitation statistics).
Any longitudinal piece, as in shipbuilding etc.
Relating to length, or to longitude.
Running in the direction of the long axis of a body.
Forward and/or backward, relative to some defined direction.
(of a study) Sampling data over time rather than merely once.
Miniature, tiny, small.
The acquisition of money
Profitable.
definitions.example: He's always working on one money-making project or another.

definitions.definition: For profit.
definitions.example: We're a moneymaking enterprise, not a charity.

definitions.definition: A unit of length equal to one twelfth of a foot, or exactly 2.54 centimetres.
The amount of water which would cover a surface to the depth of an inch, used as a measurement of rainfall.
The amount of an alcoholic beverage which would fill a glass or bottle to the depth of an inch.
A very short distance.
definitions.example: "Don't move an inch!"

definitions.definition: (followed by a preposition) To advance very slowly, or by a small amount (in a particular direction).
definitions.example: Fearful of falling, he inched along the window ledge.

definitions.definition: To drive by inches, or small degrees.
To deal out by inches; to give sparingly.
An animal character with human-like characteristics; most commonly refers to such characters created by members of the furry subculture.
definitions.example: What percentage of furries are wolves?

definitions.definition: A member of the furry fandom.
definitions.example: The furry had designed an elaborate costume.

definitions.definition: Someone who roleplays or identifies with a furry character. (Compare therianthrope.)
Covered with fur, or with something resembling fur.
definitions.example: His treatment of our furry friends was nothing short of appalling.

definitions.definition: Of or related to the furry subculture.
To compel (a person or persons) to leave.
definitions.example: Andrew was ejected from his apartment for not paying the rent.

definitions.definition: To throw out or remove forcefully.
definitions.example: In other news, a Montreal man was ejected from his car when he was involved in an accident.

definitions.definition: To compel (a sports player) to leave the field because of inappropriate behaviour.
To project oneself from an aircraft.
definitions.example: The pilot lost control of the plane and had to eject.

definitions.definition: To cause (something) to come out of a machine.
definitions.example: Press that button to eject the video tape.

definitions.definition: To come out of a machine.
definitions.example: I can't get this cassette to  eject.

definitions.definition: The appearance of Jesus Christ to the Magi on the twelfth day after Christmas.
An annual Christian feast celebrating this event.
The day of the celebration, January 6th, or sometimes (in Western Christianity), the Sunday between January 2nd and 8th.

The season or time of the Christian church year either from the Epiphany feast day to Shrove Tuesday (the day before Ash Wednesday, the start of Lent) or from the Epiphany feast day to the feast of the Presentation of Christ in the Temple.
An illuminating realization or discovery, often resulting in a personal feeling of elation, awe, or wonder.
definitions.example: It came to her in an epiphany what her life's work was to be.

definitions.definition: A manifestation or appearance of a divine or superhuman being.
Of or relating to theory; abstract; not empirical.
To make a record of information.
definitions.example: I wanted to record every detail of what happened, for the benefit of future generations.

definitions.definition: To make an audio or video recording of.
definitions.example: Within a week they had recorded both the song and the video for it.

definitions.definition: To give legal status to by making an official public record.
definitions.example: When the deed was recorded, we officially owned the house.

definitions.definition: To fix in a medium, usually in a tangible medium.
To make an audio, video, or multimedia recording.
To repeat; to practice.
To sing or repeat a tune.
To reflect; to ponder.
That has been fixed into a physical medium (for example, a book, compact disc or videotape).
definitions.example: Recorded music comes in many forms.

definitions.definition: A liquid from a plant, especially fruit.
definitions.example: Squeeze the orange and some juice will come out.

definitions.definition: A beverage made of juice.
definitions.example: I’d like two orange juices please.

definitions.definition: Any liquid resembling juice.
A soft drink.
Electricity.
Liquor.
Political power.
Petrol; gasoline.
Vitality.
The amount charged by a bookmaker for betting services.
Steroids.
Semen.
The vaginal lubrication that a woman naturally produces when sexually aroused.
Musical agreement between instrumentalists.
To extract the juice from something.
To energize or stimulate something.
The seating area at a stadium or arena; the bleachers.
The audience at a public event.
To behave dramatically or showily to impress an audience or observers; to pander to a crowd.
definitions.example: Many politicians prefer to grandstand on hot-button issues rather than enact tough policy changes.

definitions.definition: An adult male human.
definitions.example: The show is especially popular with middle-aged men.

definitions.definition: (collective) All human males collectively: mankind.
A human, a person of either gender, usually an adult. (See usage notes.)
definitions.example: every man for himself

definitions.definition: (collective) All humans collectively: mankind, humankind, humanity. (Sometimes capitalized as Man.)
A member of the genus Homo, especially of the species Homo sapiens.
An male person, usually an adult; a (generally adult male) sentient being, whether human, supernatural, elf, alien, etc.
An adult male who has, to an eminent degree, qualities considered masculine, such as strength, integrity, and devotion to family; a mensch.
Manliness; the quality or state of being manly.
A husband.
A lover; a boyfriend.
A male enthusiast or devotee; a male who is very fond of or devoted to a specified kind of thing. (Used as the last element of a compound.)
definitions.example: Some people prefer apple pie, but me, I’m a cherry pie man.

definitions.definition: A person, usually male, who has duties or skills associated with a specified thing. (Used as the last element of a compound.)
definitions.example: I wanted to be a guitar man on a road tour, but instead I’m a flag man on a road crew.

definitions.definition: A person, usually male, who can fulfill one's requirements with regard to a specified matter.
A male who belongs to a particular group: an employee, a student or alumnus, a representative, etc.
An adult male servant.
A vassal; a subject.
definitions.example: Like master, like man.

definitions.definition: A piece or token used in board games such as chess.
Used to refer to oneself or one's group: I, we; construed in the third person.
A term of familiar address often implying on the part of the speaker some degree of authority, impatience, or haste.
definitions.example: Come on, man, we've got no time to lose!

definitions.definition: A friendly term of address usually reserved for other adult males.
definitions.example: Hey, man, how's it goin'?

definitions.definition: A player on whom another is playing, with the intent of limiting their attacking impact.
A person trained in ninjutsu, especially one used for espionage, assassination, and other tasks requiring stealth during Japan's shogunate period.
A person considered similarly skillful to the historical ninja, especially in covert or stealthy operation.
A person considered to look like the historical ninja in some way, including (Mongolia) amateur private miners.
To act or move like a ninja, particularly with regard to a combination of speed, power, and stealth.
To claim an item through abuse of game mechanics.
definitions.example: That damn warrior ninja'd an epic-quality wand even though he can't even use it!

definitions.definition: To appropriate something (before someone else does).
To displace something, or take precedence over something.
To secure (land, etc.) by the right of preemption.
To make a preemptive bid at bridge.
A fine, sharp implement usually for piercing such as sewing, or knitting, acupuncture, tattooing, body piercing, medical injections, etc.
definitions.example: The seamstress threaded the needle to sew on a button.

definitions.definition: Any slender, pointed object resembling a needle, such as a pointed crystal, a sharp pinnacle of rock, an obelisk, etc.
A fine measurement indicator on a dial or graph, e.g. a compass needle.
definitions.example: The needle on the fuel gauge pointed to empty.

definitions.definition: A sensor for playing phonograph records, a phonograph stylus.
definitions.example: Ziggy bought some diamond needles for his hi-fi phonograph.

definitions.definition: A needle-like leaf found on some conifers.
A strong beam resting on props, used as a temporary support during building repairs.
(usually preceded by the) The death penalty carried out by lethal injection.
A text string that is searched for within another string. (see: needle in a haystack)
To pierce with a needle, especially for sewing or acupuncture.
To tease in order to provoke; to poke fun at.
definitions.example: Billy needled his sister incessantly about her pimples.

definitions.definition: To form, or be formed, in the shape of a needle.
definitions.example: to needle crystals

definitions.definition: A sour liquid formed by the fermentation of alcohol used as a condiment or preservative; a dilute solution of acetic acid.
Any variety of vinegar.
definitions.example: a range of herb-flavoured vinegars

definitions.definition: To season or otherwise treat with vinegar.
Someone who votes.
definitions.example: In your opinion, should we allow 16 and 17 year olds to become voters?

definitions.definition: The state of being valid, authentic or genuine.
State of having legal force.
A quality of a measurement indicating the degree to which the measure reflects the underlying construct, that is, whether it measures what it purports to measure (see reliability).
Rock that is ground more finely than gravel, but is not as fine as silt (more formally, see grain sizes chart), forming beaches and deserts and also used in construction.
(often in the plural) A beach or other expanse of sand.
definitions.example: The Canadian tar sands are a promising source of oil.

definitions.definition: (circa 1920) Personal courage.
A particle from 62.5 microns to 2 mm in diameter, following the Wentworth scale.
A light beige colour, like that of typical sand.
A single grain of sand.
A moment or interval of time; the term or extent of one's life (referring to the sand in an hourglass).
To abrade the surface of (something) with sand or sandpaper in order to smooth or clean it.
To cover with sand.
To blot ink using sand.
A sandpiper.
An artist who paints pictures.
A laborer or workman who paints surfaces using a paintbrush or other means.

A long rope or flat web line, more commonly referred to as a longe line, approximately 20-30 feet long, attached to the bridle, longeing cavesson, or halter of a horse and used to control the animal while longeing.
A lunge; a thrust.
The training ground for a horse.
To work (a horse) in a circle at the end of a long line or rope.
Someone who intrudes.
A joke.
A comic strip.
Amusing; humorous; comical.
definitions.example: When I went to the circus, I only found the clowns funny.

definitions.definition: Strange or unusual, often implying unpleasant.
definitions.example: I've got a funny feeling that this isn't going to work.

definitions.definition: Showing unexpected resentment.
In an unusual manner; strangely.
Dishonest; based on fraud or deception.
False, phony.
definitions.example: He tried to pass a fraudulent check.

definitions.definition: To make easy or easier.
To help bring about.
To preside over (a meeting, a seminar).
The process of imparting knowledge, skill and judgment.
definitions.example: 2016-06-17 AROP JOSEPH "Education is the slight hammer that breaks the yoke of ignorance, and moulds knowledge, skills, ideas, good moral values in a person be it a child, a youth or full grown adult. no matter a persons age learning never stops".

definitions.definition: Facts, skills and ideas that have been learned, either formally or informally.
definitions.example: He has had a classical education.

definitions.definition: An elderly person
(the elderly) older people as a whole
Old; having lived for relatively many years.
Of an object, being old-fashioned or frail due to aging.
The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.
definitions.example: Time stops for nobody.   the ebb and flow of time

definitions.definition: A duration of time.
An instant of time.
The measurement under some system of region of day or moment.
definitions.example: Let's synchronize our watches so we're not on different time.

definitions.definition: Ratio of comparison.
definitions.example: your car runs three times faster than mine;  that is four times as heavy as this

definitions.definition: (grammar) Tense.
definitions.example: the time of a verb

definitions.definition: The measured duration of sounds; measure; tempo; rate of movement; rhythmical division.
definitions.example: common or triple time;   the musician keeps good time.

definitions.definition: To measure or record the time, duration, or rate of.
definitions.example: I used a stopwatch to time myself running around the block.

definitions.definition: To choose when something begins or how long it lasts.
definitions.example: The President timed his speech badly, coinciding with the Super Bowl.

definitions.definition: To keep or beat time; to proceed or move in time.
To pass time; to delay.
To regulate as to time; to accompany, or agree with, in time of movement.
To measure, as in music or harmony.
Reminder by the umpire for the players to continue playing after their pause.
The umpire's call in prizefights, etc.
A call by a bartender to warn patrons that the establishment is closing and no more drinks will be served.
The act of governing
To make and administer the public policy and affairs of; to exercise sovereign authority in.
definitions.example: The old king governed the land wisely.

definitions.definition: To control the actions or behavior of; to keep under control; to restrain.
definitions.example: Govern yourselves like civilized people.

definitions.definition: To exercise a deciding or determining influence on.
definitions.example: Chance governs the outcome of many card games.

definitions.definition: To control the speed, flow etc. of; to regulate.
definitions.example: a valve that governs fuel intake

definitions.definition: To exercise political authority; to run a government.
To have or exercise a determining influence.
(grammar) To require that a certain preposition, grammatical case, etc. be used with a word; sometimes used synonymously with collocate.
A tree-dwelling marsupial, Phascolarctos cinereus, that resembles a small bear with a broad head, large ears and sharp claws, mainly found in eastern Australia.
Of sinews and tendons.
Of nerves.
The act of negating something.
A denial or contradiction.

A proposition which is the contradictory of another proposition and which can be obtained from that other proposition by the appropriately placed addition/insertion of the word "not". (Or, in symbolic logic, by prepending that proposition with the symbol for the logical operator "not".)
The logical operation which obtains such (negated) propositions.
Of or relating to a navy.
Of or relating to ships in general.
definitions.example: naval architect

definitions.definition: No particular place, noplace.
definitions.example: They went on a cruise to nowhere.

definitions.definition: Unimportant; unworthy of notice.
In no place.
definitions.example: Nowhere did the rules say anything about popcorn.

definitions.definition: To no place.
definitions.example: We sat in traffic, going nowhere.

definitions.definition: An instance of having won a competition or battle or succeeded in an effort.
definitions.example: It was a great victory on the battlefield.

definitions.definition: The condition of having succeeded in a conflict or other effort.
A winged figure representing victory, common in Roman official iconography. See Winged victory.
To achieve a victory
The quality of being vulgar.
An offensive or obscene act or expression.
A gallery, platform, or balcony, usually roofed and often partly enclosed, extending along the outside of a building.
Characterized by aggression; unjustly attacking; prone to behave in a way that involves attacking or arguing.
definitions.example: an aggressive policy, war, person, nation

definitions.definition: Of heuristics, source code optimization techniques, etc.: exploiting every opportunity to be applied.
(of a tumour or disease) That spreads quickly or extensively; virulent; malignant.
A refuge, haven or other cover or protection from something.
An institution that provides temporary housing for homeless people, battered women etc.
To provide cover from damage or harassment; to shield; to protect.
To take cover.
definitions.example: During the rainstorm, we sheltered under a tree.

definitions.definition: To pick pockets; to steal.
The act of stealing from the pocket of a passerby; the crime committed by a pickpocket.
Pornography.
A pornographic film.
definitions.example: They watched a few pornos.

definitions.definition: Pornographic
definitions.example: That's a porno film.

definitions.definition: An optical phenomenon in which light is refracted through a layer of hot air close to the ground, giving the appearance of there being refuge in the distance.
An illusion.
To cause to appear as or like a mirage.
A contiguous area of land, smaller than a continent, totally surrounded by water.
An entity surrounded by other entities that are very different from itself.
definitions.example: an island of colors on a butterfly's wing

definitions.definition: A superstructure on an aircraft carrier's deck.
A traffic island.
definitions.example: the island in the middle of a roundabout

definitions.definition: An unincorporated area wholly surrounded by one or more incorporated areas.
(grammar) A phrase from which a wh-word cannot be extracted without yielding invalid grammar.
To surround with water; make into an island.
To set, dot (as if) with islands.
To isolate.
The state of forgetting completely, of being oblivious, unconscious, unaware, as when sleeping, drunk, or dead.
definitions.example: He regularly drank himself into oblivion.

definitions.definition: The state of being completely forgotten, of being reduced to a state of non-existence, extinction, or nothingness, incl. through war and destruction. (Figuratively) for an area like hell, a wasteland.
definitions.example: Due to modern technology, many more people and much more information will not slip into oblivion, contrary to what happened throughout history until now.

definitions.definition: Amnesty.
To consign to oblivion; to efface utterly.
To excel or defeat in a game; to play better than.
definitions.example: We were outplayed at tennis, but we outplayed them at football.

definitions.definition: A group of people who are closely related to one another (by blood, marriage or adoption); kin; for example, a set of parents and their children; an immediate family.
definitions.example: Our family lives in town.

definitions.definition: An extended family; a group of people who are related to one another by blood or marriage.
definitions.example: 1915, William T. Groves, A History and Genealogy of the Groves Family in America

definitions.definition: A (close-knit) group of people related by blood, friendship, marriage, law, or custom, especially if they live or work together.
definitions.example: Our company is one big happy family.

definitions.definition: Lineage, especially an honorable one
A rank in the classification of organisms, below order and above genus; a taxon at that rank.
definitions.example: Magnolias belong to the family Magnoliaceae.

definitions.definition: Any group or aggregation of things classed together as kindred or related from possessing in common characteristics which distinguish them from other things of the same order.
definitions.example: Doliracetam is a drug from the racetam family.

definitions.definition: A group of instruments having the same basic method of tone production.
definitions.example: the brass family;  the violin family

definitions.definition: A group of languages believed to have descended from the same ancestral language.
definitions.example: the Indo-European language family;  the Afroasiatic language family

definitions.definition: Used attributively.
definitions.example: For Apocynaceae, this type of flower is a family characteristic.

definitions.definition: Suitable for children and adults.
definitions.example: It's not good for a date, it's a family restaurant.

definitions.definition: Conservative, traditional.
definitions.example: The cultural struggle is for the survival of family values against all manner of atheistic amorality.

definitions.definition: Homosexual.
definitions.example: I knew he was family when I first met him.

definitions.definition: A person belonging to a particular family; a close relative or relation.
An extended narrative poem in elevated or dignified language, celebrating the feats of a deity, demigod (heroic epic), other legend or traditional hero.
definitions.example: The Icelandic epic took all night to recite.

definitions.definition: A series of events considered appropriate to an epic.
definitions.example: The book was an epic in four volumes.

definitions.definition: In software development, a large or extended user story.
Of, or relating to, an epic.
definitions.example: Beowulf is an epic poem.

definitions.definition: Momentously heroic; grand in scale or character
definitions.example: The epic defense was rewarded with the highest military decorations

definitions.definition: Extending beyond the usual or ordinary.
definitions.example: The after-prom party was truly epic.

definitions.definition: A percussion instrument consisting of a small, usually wooden, hoop closed on one side with a drum frame and featuring jingling metal disks on the tread; it is most often held in the hand and shaken rhythmically; by extension, any frame drum.
A tambourine dove.
A kind of Provençal dance.
The music for this dance.
A place or institution for the confinement of persons held in lawful custody or detention, especially for minor offenses or with reference to some future judicial proceeding.
Confinement in a jail.
The condition created by the requirement that a horse claimed in a claiming race not be run at another track for some period of time (usually 30 days).
In dodgeball and related games, the area where players who have been struck by the ball are confined.
(FreeBSD) A kind of sandbox for running a guest operating system instance.
To imprison.
A kitten or young cat.
(sometimes capitalized) A pet name for a cat.
definitions.example: Aw, look at the kitty!

definitions.definition: A money pool, as for a card game, or for shared expenses.
In a home game, a small, specified amount taken from each pot to pay the host's expenses.
Female genitals. (Euphemistic form of pussy.)
A set of additional cards dealt face down in some games.
To explode; to blow up. Specifically, to combust supersonically via shock compression.
To cause to explode.
A newcomer, someone new to something.
A new user or participant; someone who is extremely new and inexperienced (to a game or activity). A beginner.
Anything recently introduced into a setting, especially something that replaces an older version.
One who cheats.
An improvised breaker bar made from a length of pipe and a wrench (spanner), usually used to free screws, bolts, etc. that are difficult to remove with a ratchet or wrench alone.
An escheater.
Non-prescription spectacles that magnify.
The sense or ability of sight.
Something seen; an object perceived visually.
Something imaginary one thinks one sees.
definitions.example: He tried drinking from the pool of water, but realized it was only a vision.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Something unreal or imaginary; a creation of fancy.
An ideal or a goal toward which one aspires.
definitions.example: He worked tirelessly toward his vision of world peace.

definitions.definition: A religious or mystical experience of a supernatural appearance.
definitions.example: He had a vision of the Virgin Mary.

definitions.definition: A person or thing of extraordinary beauty.
Pre-recorded film or tape; footage.
To imagine something as if it were to be true.
To present as in a vision.
To provide with a vision.
A mischievous child.
A street urchin, a child who lives, or spends most of their time, in the streets.
A sea urchin.
One of a pair in a series of small card cylinders arranged around a carding drum; so called from its fancied resemblance to the hedgehog.
A neutron-generating device that triggered the nuclear detonation of the earliest plutonium atomic bombs.
A hedgehog.
A mischievous elf supposed sometimes to take the form of a hedgehog.
(in negative or interrogative constructions) From a given time onwards; longer, again.
definitions.example: They don't make repairable radios any more.

definitions.definition: (in positive constructions) Now, from now on.
definitions.example: I eat fish anymore.

definitions.definition: The act of storing goods; the state of being stored.
definitions.example: There's a lot of storage space in the loft.

definitions.definition: (usually countable) An object or place in which something is stored.
(usually uncountable) Any computer device, including such as a disk, on which data is stored for a longer term than memory.
definitions.example: I′d recommend backing up these files to storage before reinstalling the operating system.

definitions.definition: The price charged for storing goods.
To put into storage; to store.
The fine particles which are the result of reducing dry substance by pounding, grinding, or triturating, or the result of decay; dust.
A mixture of fine dry, sweet-smelling particles applied to the face or other body parts, to reduce shine or to alleviate chaffing.
An explosive mixture used in gunnery, blasting, etc.; gunpowder.
To reduce to fine particles; to pound, grind, or rub into a powder.
To sprinkle with powder, or as if with powder.
definitions.example: to powder one's hair

definitions.definition: To use powder on the hair or skin.
To turn into powder; to become powdery.
To sprinkle with salt; to corn, as meat.
A very pale blue colour.
Freshly fallen, uncompacted snow, especially in the context of Alpine skiing
definitions.example: Expert skiers love skiing on powder snow.

definitions.definition: A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.
definitions.example: Tigers and lions share a common ancestor from a few million years ago.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any of various extant and extinct big cats, especially the mountain lion.
A Chinese foo dog.
An individual who shows strength and courage, attributes associated with the lion.
A famous person regarded with interest and curiosity.
A light brown color that resembles the fur of a lion.
An old Scottish coin, with a lion on the obverse, worth 74 shillings.
Of the light brown color that resembles the fur of a lion.
Something which has mutated, which has one or more new characteristics from a mutation.
definitions.example: Your plaid fish are mutants.

definitions.definition: Someone or something that seems strange, abnormal, or bizarre.
definitions.example: Your neighbor seems to be a mutant; he’s so cringy!

definitions.definition: An object in a program that serves as a lock, used to negotiate mutual exclusion among threads.
A theatrical work, combining drama, music, song and sometimes dance.
The score for such a work.
A building designed for the performance of such works; an opera house.
A company dedicated to performing such works.
(by extension) Any showy, melodramatic or unrealistic production resembling an opera.
The rising or ascent of an aircraft or rocket into flight.
definitions.example: The flight was smooth, but the takeoff was a little rough.

definitions.definition: A parody or lampoon of someone or something.
A quantification, especially of building materials.
definitions.example: I'll give you an estimate after I do the quantity takeoffs for the trusses and structural steel.

definitions.definition: The removal of sheets from the press.
The spot from which one takes off; specifically, the place from which a jumper rises in leaping.
Any land or marine reptile of the order Testudines, characterised by a protective shell enclosing its body. See also tortoise.
(specifically) A marine reptile of that order.
An Ancient Roman attack method, where the shields held by the soldiers hide them, not only left, right, front and back, but also from above.
A type of robot having a domed case (and so resembling the reptile), used in education, especially for making line drawings by means of a computer program.
An on-screen cursor that serves the same function as a turtle for drawing.
The curved plate in which the form is held in a type-revolving cylinder press.
A small element towards the end of a list of items to be bubble sorted, and thus tending to take a long time to be swapped into its correct position. Compare rabbit.
A breakdancing move consisting of a float during which the dancer's weight shifts from one hand to the other, producing rotation or a circular "walk".
A low stand for a lamp etc.
To flip over onto the back or top; to turn upside down.
To turn and swim upside down.
To hunt turtles, especially in the water.
To build up a large defense force and strike only punctually, rather than going for an offensive strategy.
A turtle dove.
A large celebratory meal; a feast.
A dessert; a course of sweetmeats.
To participate in a banquet; to feast.
To have dessert after a feast.
To treat with a banquet or sumptuous entertainment of food; to feast.
An evergreen plant, Calluna vulgaris, with spiky leaves and small purple, pink, or white flowers.
The Ericaceae family.
Various species of the genus Erica.
Various species of the genus Cassiope.
A purple colour with a tint of pink and blue.
The use of interwoven yarns of mixed colours to produce flecks.
Of a purple colour with a tint with pink and blue.
A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.
A hard-shelled seed of this fruit, having white flesh and a fluid-filled central cavity.
The edible white flesh of this fruit.
The coconut palm.
A Hispanic or dark-skinned person who acts “white” (Caucasian), alluding to the fact that a coconut is brown on the outside and white on the inside.
A black person who thinks or acts "white" (European).
A Pacific islander.
A female breast.
The human head (often used in cricket broadcasts when a ball hits or nearly hits a batsman on the head).
To make someone a victim or sacrifice.
To punish someone unjustly.
To swindle or defraud someone.
One from whom a person is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any distance of time; a progenitor; a forefather.
An earlier type; a progenitor
definitions.example: This fossil animal is regarded as the ancestor of the horse.

definitions.definition: One from whom an estate has descended;—the correlative of heir.
One who had the same role or function in former times.
A word or phrase which serves as the origin of a term in another language.
To be an ancestor of.
A game (originally Chinese) for four players, using a collection of tiles divided into five or six suits.
A solitaire game using the same tiles, where the player wins by removing pairs of matching exposed tiles until none remain.
